questions.question_text: A particle moves along a straight line such that its displacement at any time t is given by s = t³ - 6t² + 3t + 4. The velocity when the acceleration is zero is:
A ball is thrown vertically upward with velocity 20 m/s from the top of a building 40 m high. The time after which the ball hits the ground is (g = 10 m/s²):
questions.solution_text: -40 = 20t - 5t². 5t² - 20t - 40 = 0. t² - 4t - 8 = 0. t = (4+√48)/2 = 4s (taking positive root)

questions.question_text: Two blocks of masses 5 kg and 10 kg are connected by a string passing over a frictionless pulley. The acceleration of the system is (g = 10 m/s²):
A body of mass 2 kg has kinetic energy 8 J. A constant force of 4 N is applied on it in the direction of motion. The kinetic energy after 3 m displacement is:
questions.solution_text: Work = Fd = 4×3 = 12J. Final KE = initial KE + Work = 8 + 12 = 20 J

questions.question_text: Two point charges +3μC and -3μC are placed 20 cm apart. The electric potential at the midpoint of the line joining them is:
questions.solution_text: At midpoint, distance from each charge = 10 cm. V = kq₁/r + kq₂/r = k(3-3)/0.1 = 0

questions.question_text: For N₂(g) + 3H₂(g) ⇌ 2NH₃(g), if Kp = 1.6 × 10⁻⁴ at 400°C, find Kc. (R = 0.0821). Answer in × 10⁻².
How many moles of electrons are required for the reduction of 1 mole of Cr₂O₇²⁻ to Cr³⁺?
questions.solution_text: Cr₂O₇²⁻ → 2Cr³⁺. Each Cr goes from +6 to +3 (gains 3e⁻). Two Cr atoms → 6 electrons total.

questions.question_text: Which of the following carbocations is most stable?
questions.solution_text: Tertiary carbocation (CH₃)₃C⁺ is most stable due to +I effect of 3 methyl groups and hyperconjugation.

questions.question_text: Among Li, Na, K, Rb, which has the highest ionization energy?
questions.solution_text: IE decreases down a group. Li is smallest, most electronegative → highest IE in Group 1.

questions.question_text: For a first-order reaction, the half-life is 693 seconds. The rate constant is:
questions.solution_text: For first order: t₁/₂ = 0.693/k → k = 0.693/693 = 10⁻³ s⁻¹

questions.question_text: If f(x) = x³ - 3x² + 3x - 1, which of the following are true?
questions.options: ["A) f(1) = 0","B) f'(1) = 0","C) f''(1) = 0","D) f has a point of inflection at x=1"]

questions.question_text: If α, β are roots of x² - 5x + 6 = 0, the value of α³ + β³ is:
The value of ∫₀^π sin²x dx is:
questions.solution_text: ∫sin²x dx = ∫(1-cos2x)/2 dx = x/2 - sin2x/4. At π: π/2 - 0 = π/2. At 0: 0. Answer = π/2

questions.question_text: If sin A + sin B = 1 and cos A + cos B = 0, then the value of 12 cos 2A + 4 cos 2B is:
A bag contains 5 red and 3 blue balls. Two balls are drawn at random without replacement. The probability that both are red is:
An ideal gas undergoes an isothermal expansion at 300 K. If the volume doubles, the work done by 2 moles of gas is (R = 8.314 J/mol·K):
A convex lens of focal length 20 cm produces a real image that is 3 times the size of the object. The object distance is:
questions.solution_text: For real image m = -v/u = -3, so v = 3u (but v is positive). u = -u₀, v = 3u₀. 1/(3u₀) + 1/u₀ = 1/20. 4/(3u₀) = 1/20. u₀ = 80/3 cm.

questions.question_text: A projectile is launched at 60° to the horizontal with speed 40 m/s. The maximum height reached is ___ m. (Take g = 10 m/s²)
Three capacitors of 2μF, 3μF, and 6μF are connected in series. The equivalent capacitance in μF is ___.
A block of mass 10 kg is placed on a rough inclined plane of inclination 30°. If μ = 1/√3, the force required to just move the block up the incline is:
The rate of a reaction doubles when temperature increases from 27°C to 37°C. The activation energy of the reaction is approximately:
questions.solution_text: Using ln(k₂/k₁) = Ea/R × (1/T₁ - 1/T₂). ln2 = Ea/8.314 × (1/300 - 1/310). Ea ≈ 53.6 kJ/mol

questions.question_text: The correct order of decreasing acidity of the following compounds is: (I) Phenol (II) p-Nitrophenol (III) p-Methylphenol (IV) p-Chlorophenol
questions.options: ["A) II > IV > I > III","B) II > I > IV > III","C) IV > II > I > III","D) I > II > III > IV"]
questions.solution_text: p-NO₂ (strong -M,-I) > p-Cl (-I) > H > p-CH₃ (+I, decreases acidity). So II > IV > I > III.

questions.question_text: How many grams of NaOH (molar mass = 40 g/mol) are needed to prepare 500 mL of 0.1 M solution?
Which of the following has the highest electron affinity?
questions.solution_text: Cl has higher EA than F because F is too small — incoming e⁻ faces inter-electronic repulsion. Cl has optimal size.

questions.question_text: The pH of 0.001 M HCl solution is:
questions.solution_text: HCl is a strong acid, fully ionized. [H⁺] = 0.001 = 10⁻³. pH = -log(10⁻³) = 3

questions.question_text: The length of the latus rectum of the ellipse 9x² + 25y² = 225 is:
questions.solution_text: Dividing by 225: x²/25 + y²/9 = 1. So a²=25, b²=9. LR = 2b²/a = 2(9)/5 = 18/5

questions.question_text: If A is a 3×3 matrix with |A| = 5, then |3A| is ___.
questions.solution_text: For n×n matrix, |kA| = kⁿ|A|. So |3A| = 3³ × 5 = 27 × 5 = 135

questions.question_text: The function f(x) = x³ - 3x has a local minimum at:
The value of ∫₀¹ (3x² + 2x) dx is ___.
questions.solution_text: ∫(3x²+2x)dx = x³ + x². At x=1: 1+1=2. At x=0: 0. Answer = 2

questions.question_text: If P(A) = 0.6, P(B) = 0.4, and A and B are independent, then P(A ∩ B) is:
questions.solution_text: For independent events: P(A∩B) = P(A) × P(B) = 0.6 × 0.4 = 0.24

questions.question_text: A stone is thrown vertically upwards with a speed of 20 m/s from the top of a building of height 25 m. The speed with which it hits the ground is (g = 10 m/s²):
A body of mass 2 kg moving with velocity 4 m/s makes a head-on elastic collision with a body of mass 6 kg at rest. The velocity of the 2 kg body after collision is:
A solid sphere of mass 1 kg and radius 10 cm rolls without slipping with velocity 2 m/s. Its total KE in joules is ___.
In a Wheatstone bridge, if P = 100Ω, Q = 1000Ω, S = 100Ω, for balanced condition R is:
questions.solution_text: P/Q = R/S → R = PS/Q = 10 Ω

questions.question_text: A convex lens of focal length 20 cm produces a real image 3× the object size. Object distance is:

questions.options: ["A) Kp = Kc(RT)⁻²","B) Kp = Kc(RT)²","C) Kp = Kc","D) Kp = Kc(RT)⁻¹"]

questions.question_text: Which undergoes SN1 reaction fastest?
questions.solution_text: 3° halide forms most stable carbocation → fastest SN1.

questions.question_text: For a first-order reaction, 75% consumed in 32 min. Half-life in minutes is ___.
questions.solution_text: 25% left = (½)², so 2 half-lives = 32 → t½ = 16

questions.solution_text: Be has fully filled 2s² → extra stability → higher IE.

[PYQ] [2024] A uniform solid cylinder of mass 2 kg rolls without slipping. If its cm velocity is 10 m/s, the total kinetic energy is:
questions.solution_text: Total KE = Translational KE + Rotational KE = (1/2)mv² + (1/2)Iω². For a solid cylinder, I = (1/2)mR² and ω = v/R. Thus, Total KE = (3/4)mv² = 0.75mv².

questions.question_text: [PYQ] [2023] In YDSE, slit separation is 0.2 mm, screen is 1.0 m away. Wavelength 450 nm. The fringe width is:
questions.solution_text: Fringe width β = λD/d. Substituting the given values for wavelength λ, distance D, and slit separation d gives the fringe width.

questions.question_text: [PYQ] [2024] The work function of a metal is 2.4 eV. Incident light has energy 6.0 eV. Maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons is:
questions.solution_text: By Einstein photoelectric equation, K_max = E - Φ. Subtracting the work function from incident energy gives the max KE.

questions.question_text: [PYQ] [2022] A Carnot engine works between 530 K and 330 K. Its efficiency is:
questions.solution_text: Efficiency η = 1 - (T_cold / T_hot). Substitute the absolute temperatures to find efficiency.

questions.solution_text: When a dielectric of constant K is introduced, the new capacitance becomes C = K * C_original.

questions.question_text: [PYQ] [2023] If A is a 3x3 matrix with |A| = 21, then |2A| is:
questions.solution_text: For an n×n matrix, |kA| = k^n * |A|. Since A is 3x3, |2A| = 2³ * |A| = 8 * |A|.

questions.question_text: [PYQ] [2024] In a GP, the third term is 4 and the product of the first five terms is X. Find log_(4+i) X.
questions.solution_text: Product = ar^0 * ar^1 * ar^2 * ar^3 * ar^4 = a^5 r^10 = (ar^2)^5. Since 3rd term is ar^2, product is (3rd term)^5.

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2020] A block of mass 2kg on a frictionless inclined plane of angle 30° is connected via a string over a pulley to a hanging block. If the system accelerates at g/10, find the mass of the hanging block (in kg).
questions.solution_text: mg - T = ma, T - Mgsinθ = Ma → solve simultaneously. m(g-a)=M(a+gsinθ), m=2(g/10+gsin30)/(g-g/10)=2(0.6g)/(0.9g)=1.2 kg

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2019] A particle of mass m is projected with velocity v₀ at angle 45° with horizontal. The magnitude of angular momentum about the point of projection when the particle is at maximum height is:
questions.solution_text: At max height: x=R/2=v₀²sin90°/(2g), vy=0, vx=v₀cos45°. L=m·vx·H=m·(v₀/√2)·(v₀²sin²45°/2g)=mv₀³/(4√2·g)

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2021] Two identical conducting spheres A and B carry charges +Q and -3Q. They are separated by distance d. They are brought into contact and then separated to distance d. The ratio of force after contact to before contact is:
questions.solution_text: Before: F₁=kQ·3Q/d²=3kQ²/d². After contact: each has (-3Q+Q)/2=-Q. F₂=kQ²/d². Ratio=1:3

questions.solution_text: Using energy conservation: Mg(L/2)=½Iω², I=ML²/3. ω=√(3g/L)

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2020] A satellite is in a circular orbit of radius R around Earth. The minimum energy required to transfer it to an orbit of radius 2R is:
questions.solution_text: E₁=-GMm/2R, E₂=-GMm/4R. ΔE=E₂-E₁=GMm/4R

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2019] The number of sp² hybridized carbon atoms in CH₂=CH-C≡C-CH=CH₂ is:
questions.solution_text: CH₂=CH (2 sp²) and CH=CH₂ (2 sp²). The C≡C carbons are sp. Total sp²=4

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2020] Which of the following are aromatic? (Choose all correct)
questions.options: ["A) Cyclopentadienyl anion","B) Cycloheptatrienyl cation","C) Cyclooctatetraene","D) Benzene"]
questions.solution_text: Aromatic requires 4n+2 π electrons, planar, cyclic. C₅H₅⁻ has 6π (A✓), C₇H₇⁺ has 6π (B✓), COT has 8π=4×2 antiaromatic (C✗), benzene 6π (D✓)

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2021] The standard electrode potential of Cu²⁺/Cu is +0.34V and Cu⁺/Cu is +0.52V. The standard electrode potential of Cu²⁺/Cu⁺ is (in V):

questions.solution_text: Ligands in alphabetical order: ammine before chlorido. Co is +3. Answer: Tetraamminedichloridocobalt(III) chloride

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2023] The major product of reaction of 2-methylpropene with HBr in the presence of peroxide is:
questions.solution_text: With peroxides: anti-Markovnikov addition occurs. Br adds to less substituted carbon → 1-bromo-2-methylpropane

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2020] The number of geometrical isomers possible for [MA₂B₂] in square planar geometry is:
questions.solution_text: Square planar MA₂B₂ has cis and trans isomers = 2 geometrical isomers

questions.solution_text: E°cell=E°cathode-E°anode=0.34-(-0.76)=1.10V

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2024] The hybridization of central atom in XeF₄ is:
questions.solution_text: XeF₄: 4 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs = 6 electron pairs → sp³d² hybridization

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2019] The number of solutions of the equation sin(x)+sin(3x)+sin(5x)=0 in [0,π] is:
questions.solution_text: sin(x)+sin(5x)+sin(3x)=0 → 2sin(3x)cos(2x)+sin(3x)=0 → sin(3x)[2cos(2x)+1]=0. sin(3x)=0: x=0,π/3,2π/3,π. cos(2x)=-1/2: 2x=2π/3,4π/3 → x=π/3,2π/3. Total distinct: 0,π/3,2π/3,π + from cos: already counted. Need to recheck. Answer: 5 solutions

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2020] If z=x+iy and |z-1|=|z+1|, then z lies on:
questions.solution_text: |z-1|=|z+1| → distance from (1,0) = distance from (-1,0) → perpendicular bisector of segment joining them → y-axis (x=0)

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2021] The value of ∫₀^π x·sin²(x) dx is:
questions.solution_text: Using I=∫₀^π x·sin²x dx. By property: I=∫₀^π (π-x)sin²x dx. 2I=π∫₀^π sin²x dx=π·π/2. I=π²/4

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2022] Which of the following matrices are invertible? (Choose all correct)
questions.solution_text: Invertible iff det≠0. A: det=4-6=-2≠0 (✓). B: det=4-4=0 (✗). C: det=0-1=-1≠0 (✓). D: det=0 (✗)

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2023] The sum of the series 1+1/2²+1/3²+1/4²+... converges to:
questions.solution_text: Basel problem: Σ(1/n²)=π²/6 ≈ 1.6449

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2019] The area enclosed between the curves y=x² and y=√x is:
questions.solution_text: Intersection: x²=√x → x⁴=x → x(x³-1)=0 → x=0,1. Area=∫₀¹(√x-x²)dx=[2x^(3/2)/3-x³/3]₀¹=2/3-1/3=1/3≈0.33

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2020] The number of ways to distribute 10 identical balls into 3 distinct boxes such that no box is empty is:
questions.solution_text: Stars and bars with restriction: C(10-1,3-1)=C(9,2)=36

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2023] Which of the following functions are differentiable at x=0? (Choose all)
questions.solution_text: |x| has corner at 0 (A✗). x|x|=x² for x≥0, -x² for x<0, f'(0)=0 (B✓). x²sin(1/x) → f'(0)=lim h·sin(1/h)=0 (C✓). x³ clearly (D✓)

questions.question_text: [JEE Adv 2024] The general solution of dy/dx + y·tanx = secx is:
questions.options: ["A) y·cosx = x + C","B) y·secx = x + C","C) y·sinx = x + C","D) y = sinx + C·cosx"]
questions.solution_text: Linear ODE. IF=e^(∫tanx dx)=secx. y·secx=∫sec²x dx=tanx+C. y=sinx+C·cosx

resources.title: Kinematics — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 KINEMATICS — STUDY OF MOTION

🔑 Key Equations of Motion:
• v = u + at
• s = ut + ½at²
• v² = u² + 2as
• s = ½(u+v)t

Where: u = initial velocity, v = final velocity, a = acceleration, t = time, s = displacement

📐 Projectile Motion:
• Time of flight: T = 2u·sinθ/g
• Max height: H = u²sin²θ/2g
• Range: R = u²sin2θ/g
• Max range at θ = 45°

📊 Graph Analysis:
• Slope of x-t graph = velocity
• Slope of v-t graph = acceleration
• Area under v-t graph = displacement

⚡ Relative Motion:
• v_AB = v_A - v_B
• Rain-man problems: use vector addition

resources.title: Kinematics — Formula Sheet
resources.content:
📋 KINEMATICS FORMULAS

1D Motion:
  v = u + at
  s = ut + ½at²
  v² = u² + 2as
  s_nth = u + a(2n-1)/2

2D Projectile:
  x = u·cosθ·t
  y = u·sinθ·t - ½gt²
  T = 2u·sinθ/g
  H = u²sin²θ/(2g)
  R = u²sin2θ/g

Circular Motion:
  v = rω
  a_c = v²/r = rω²
  T = 2π/ω

resources.title: Newton's Laws — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 NEWTON'S LAWS OF MOTION

1st Law (Inertia): Body remains at rest or uniform motion unless acted on by external force.

2nd Law: F = ma (net force = mass × acceleration)

3rd Law: Every action has an equal and opposite reaction.

📐 Free Body Diagram Steps:
1. Isolate the body
2. Draw all forces (gravity, normal, friction, tension, applied)
3. Choose coordinate axes
4. Resolve forces into components
5. Apply ΣFx = max, ΣFy = may

🔥 Friction:
• Static: f ≤ μₛN
• Kinetic: f = μₖN
• μₛ > μₖ always

📊 Connected Bodies:
• Same string → same tension (massless string)
• Same surface → same acceleration

resources.title: Laws of Motion — Formulas
resources.content:
📋 LAWS OF MOTION FORMULAS

F = ma
W = mg
f_s ≤ μₛN
f_k = μₖN
T = m(g±a) [lift problems]
Pulley: a = (m₁-m₂)g/(m₁+m₂)
Banking: tanθ = v²/(rg)

resources.title: Work, Energy & Power — Notes
resources.content:
📖 WORK, ENERGY & POWER

Work: W = F·d·cosθ
• Positive work: θ < 90°
• Negative work: θ > 90°
• Zero work: θ = 90°

Kinetic Energy: KE = ½mv²
Potential Energy: PE = mgh (gravity), ½kx² (spring)

Work-Energy Theorem: W_net = ΔKE

Conservation of Energy: KE + PE = constant (conservative forces)

Power: P = W/t = F·v

Collisions:
• Elastic: KE conserved, e = 1
• Inelastic: KE not conserved, e < 1
• Perfectly inelastic: Bodies stick, e = 0

resources.title: Electrostatics — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 ELECTROSTATICS

Coulomb's Law: F = kq₁q₂/r²  (k = 9×10⁹ Nm²/C²)

Electric Field: E = F/q = kQ/r²
Field due to:
• Point charge: E = kQ/r²
• Infinite line: E = 2kλ/r
• Infinite plane: E = σ/(2ε₀)

Electric Potential: V = kQ/r
Relation: E = -dV/dr

Gauss's Law: Φ = q_enclosed/ε₀

Capacitance: C = Q/V
• Parallel plate: C = ε₀A/d
• Series: 1/C = 1/C₁ + 1/C₂
• Parallel: C = C₁ + C₂
• With dielectric: C = Kε₀A/d

resources.title: Thermodynamics — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 THERMODYNAMICS

1st Law: ΔQ = ΔU + ΔW

Processes:
• Isothermal (T=const): W = nRT·ln(V₂/V₁)
• Adiabatic (Q=0): PVᵞ = const
• Isobaric (P=const): W = PΔV
• Isochoric (V=const): W = 0

Carnot Efficiency: η = 1 - T_cold/T_hot
Cp - Cv = R
γ = Cp/Cv

resources.title: Ray Optics — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 RAY OPTICS

Mirror Formula: 1/v + 1/u = 1/f
Lens Formula: 1/v - 1/u = 1/f
Snell's Law: n₁sinθ₁ = n₂sinθ₂
Critical Angle: sinC = n₂/n₁
Magnification: m = -v/u (mirror), v/u (lens)
Power: P = 1/f (in metres) → unit: Dioptre
Lensmaker's: 1/f = (n-1)(1/R₁ - 1/R₂)

resources.title: Rotational Motion — Notes
resources.content:
📖 ROTATIONAL MOTION

🔑 Moment of Inertia (I):
• Point mass: I = mr²
• Rod (centre): ML²/12, Rod (end): ML²/3
• Disc: MR²/2, Ring: MR²
• Solid sphere: 2MR²/5, Hollow sphere: 2MR²/3

📐 Parallel Axis: I = I_cm + Md²
Perpendicular Axis: I_z = I_x + I_y (planar)

Torque: τ = r × F = Iα
Angular Momentum: L = Iω
Conservation: If τ_ext = 0, L = constant

🔄 Rolling: v_cm = Rω, KE = ½mv² + ½Iω²
Acceleration on incline: a = gsinθ/(1+I/mR²)

resources.title: Rotational Motion — Formulas
resources.content:
📋 ROTATIONAL FORMULAS

ω = ω₀ + αt
θ = ω₀t + ½αt²
ω² = ω₀² + 2αθ
τ = Iα = r×F
L = Iω = r×p
KE_rot = ½Iω²
Rolling: KE = ½mv²(1+k²/R²)

resources.title: Gravitation — Notes
resources.content:
📖 GRAVITATION

Newton Law: F = GMm/r²
g = GM/R² (surface)
At height h: g_h = g(1-2h/R) for h<<R
At depth d: g_d = g(1-d/R)

Gravitational PE: U = -GMm/r
Escape Velocity: v_e = √(2gR) = 11.2 km/s
Orbital Velocity: v_o = √(gR)

Kepler Laws:
1. Elliptical orbits, Sun at focus
2. Equal areas in equal time
3. T² ∝ a³

Satellite: T = 2π√(r³/GM)
Binding Energy = GMm/(2r)

resources.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases — Notes
resources.content:
📖 KINETIC THEORY OF GASES

Ideal Gas: PV = nRT
Pressure: P = (1/3)ρv²_rms
KE per molecule = (3/2)kT
KE per mole = (3/2)RT

v_rms = √(3RT/M)
v_mp = √(2RT/M)
v_avg = √(8RT/πM)

Degrees of Freedom:
• Monoatomic: f=3, γ=5/3
• Diatomic: f=5, γ=7/5
• Polyatomic: f=6, γ=4/3

U = (f/2)nRT
Mean free path: λ = 1/(√2·π·d²·n)

resources.title: Heat Transfer — Notes
resources.content:
📖 HEAT TRANSFER

Conduction: Q/t = KA(T₁-T₂)/L
R_thermal = L/(KA)
Series: R = R₁+R₂, Parallel: 1/R = 1/R₁+1/R₂

Convection: Newton law of cooling
dT/dt = -k(T - T₀)

Radiation:
Stefan Law: E = σT⁴
Net: P = σA(T⁴ - T₀⁴)
Wien Law: λ_max·T = b (b=2.9×10⁻³ m·K)

resources.title: Simple Harmonic Motion — Notes
resources.content:
📖 SIMPLE HARMONIC MOTION

a = -ω²x
x = Asin(ωt+φ)
v = Aωcos(ωt+φ) = ω√(A²-x²)
a = -Aω²sin(ωt+φ)

Time Period:
• Spring: T = 2π√(m/k)
• Pendulum: T = 2π√(L/g)
• Physical: T = 2π√(I/mgd)

Energy:
• KE = ½mω²(A²-x²)
• PE = ½mω²x²
• Total E = ½mω²A² = constant

Springs:
Series: 1/k = 1/k₁+1/k₂
Parallel: k = k₁+k₂

resources.title: SHM — Formula Sheet
resources.content:
📋 SHM FORMULAS

x = Asin(ωt+φ), v = ω√(A²-x²), a = -ω²x
T = 2π/ω, f = 1/T
E = ½kA², v_max = Aω, a_max = Aω²
T_spring = 2π√(m/k), T_pendulum = 2π√(L/g)

resources.title: Wave Motion — Notes
resources.content:
📖 WAVE MOTION

y = Asin(kx-ωt), k=2π/λ, ω=2πf, v=fλ

Speed: String v=√(T/μ), Gas v=√(γRT/M)

Superposition & Interference:
• Constructive: path diff = nλ
• Destructive: path diff = (2n+1)λ/2

Standing Waves:
• Strings: f_n = nv/(2L)
• Beats: f_beat = |f₁-f₂|

resources.title: Sound Waves — Notes
resources.content:
📖 SOUND WAVES

Longitudinal wave, v ≈ 343 m/s in air

Organ Pipes:
Closed: f_n = (2n-1)v/(4L) — odd harmonics only
Open: f_n = nv/(2L) — all harmonics

Doppler Effect:
f' = f(v±v_obs)/(v∓v_src)
Source approaching → f↑

Intensity: I = P/(4πr²)
Loudness: β = 10log₁₀(I/I₀) dB

resources.title: Current Electricity — Notes
resources.content:
📖 CURRENT ELECTRICITY

Ohm Law: V = IR, R = ρL/A
Series: R = R₁+R₂+R₃
Parallel: 1/R = 1/R₁+1/R₂+1/R₃

Kirchhoff Laws:
• KCL: ΣI_in = ΣI_out
• KVL: ΣV = 0 around loop

EMF: V = E - Ir
Power: P = VI = I²R = V²/R

Wheatstone Bridge: P/Q = R/S
Meter Bridge: R = S(100-l)/l
Potentiometer: E₁/E₂ = l₁/l₂

resources.title: Magnetic Effects — Notes
resources.content:
📖 MAGNETIC EFFECTS OF CURRENT

Biot-Savart: dB = (μ₀/4π)(Idl×r̂)/r²

Fields:
• Long wire: B = μ₀I/(2πr)
• Loop centre: B = μ₀I/(2R)
• Solenoid: B = μ₀nI

Ampere Law: ∮B·dl = μ₀I_enc
Force: F = IL×B
Parallel wires: F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/(2πd)

Lorentz Force: F = q(v×B)
Circular motion: r = mv/(qB), T = 2πm/(qB)
Magnetic moment: M = NIA, τ = M×B

resources.title: Electromagnetic Induction — Notes
resources.content:
📖 ELECTROMAGNETIC INDUCTION

Faraday Law: EMF = -dΦ/dt
Φ = BAcosθ
Lenz Law: opposes change

Motional EMF: ε = Blv
Self Inductance: ε = -LdI/dt
Solenoid: L = μ₀n²Al
Energy: U = ½LI²

AC Circuits:
V = V₀sin(ωt), V_rms = V₀/√2
Z = √(R²+(X_L-X_C)²)
X_L = ωL, X_C = 1/(ωC)
Resonance: ω = 1/√(LC)
Power factor: cosφ = R/Z

resources.title: Wave Optics — Notes
resources.content:
📖 WAVE OPTICS

YDSE:
Bright: dsinθ = nλ
Dark: dsinθ = (2n+1)λ/2
Fringe width: β = λD/d

Single Slit: Central max = 2λD/a
Minima: asinθ = nλ

Polarization:
Malus Law: I = I₀cos²θ
Brewster: tanθ_B = n

Optical Path = μ × geometric path

resources.title: Dual Nature of Matter — Notes
resources.content:
📖 DUAL NATURE OF MATTER

Photoelectric: E = hf = hc/λ
KE_max = hf - φ
Stopping potential: eV₀ = KE_max
Threshold: f₀ = φ/h

de Broglie: λ = h/p = h/(mv)
For electron: λ = 12.27/√V Å

h = 6.63×10⁻³⁴ J·s
1 eV = 1.6×10⁻¹⁹ J

resources.title: Atoms & Nuclei — Notes
resources.content:
📖 ATOMS & NUCLEI

Bohr Model:
r_n = 0.529n² Å
E_n = -13.6/n² eV
1/λ = R(1/n₁² - 1/n₂²)

Lyman(UV n→1), Balmer(Vis n→2), Paschen(IR n→3)

Nuclear:
Δm = Zm_p + Nm_n - M
BE = Δmc²

Radioactivity:
N = N₀e^(-λt)
T½ = 0.693/λ
α: Z-2,A-4  β⁻: Z+1  γ: no change

resources.title: Semiconductor Electronics — Notes
resources.content:
📖 SEMICONDUCTORS

Intrinsic: pure Si/Ge, n_e = n_h
n-type: pentavalent dopant (P, As)
p-type: trivalent dopant (B, Al)

p-n Junction:
Forward bias: current flows, V > 0.7V
Reverse bias: tiny current
Breakdown: Zener / Avalanche

Transistor: I_E = I_B + I_C
β = I_C/I_B

Applications: Rectifier, Zener regulator, LED, Solar cell
Logic Gates: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR

resources.title: Mole Concept — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 MOLE CONCEPT

1 mole = 6.022 × 10²³ particles (Avogadro's number)

n = mass/molar mass = PV/RT (ideal gas)

Empirical vs Molecular formula:
• Empirical = simplest ratio
• Molecular = actual formula

Stoichiometry:
1. Balance the equation
2. Convert given to moles
3. Use mole ratio
4. Convert to required unit

% Composition = (mass of element/molar mass) × 100

Limiting Reagent: reagent that gets consumed first

resources.title: Organic Chemistry Basics — Notes
resources.content:
📖 GENERAL ORGANIC CHEMISTRY

Hybridization:
• sp³ → tetrahedral (109.5°)
• sp² → trigonal planar (120°)
• sp → linear (180°)

Electronic Effects:
• Inductive: +I (electron donating), -I (withdrawing)
• Resonance/Mesomeric: +M, -M
• Hyperconjugation: no-bond resonance

Reaction Intermediates:
• Carbocation: sp², planar, electrophilic
  Stability: 3° > 2° > 1° > CH₃⁺
• Carbanion: sp³, pyramidal, nucleophilic
• Free radical: sp², planar

Acidity: -I and -M groups increase acidity
Basicity: +I and +M groups increase basicity

resources.title: Chemical Equilibrium — Notes
resources.content:
📖 CHEMICAL EQUILIBRIUM

Law of Mass Action: Kc = [Products]/[Reactants]

Relation: Kp = Kc(RT)^Δn

Le Chatelier's Principle:
• Increase conc → shifts away
• Increase pressure → shifts to fewer moles
• Increase temp → shifts to endothermic side

Ionic Equilibrium:
pH = -log[H⁺]
pOH = -log[OH⁻]
pH + pOH = 14
Ka × Kb = Kw = 10⁻¹⁴

Buffer: pH = pKa + log([salt]/[acid])

resources.title: Periodic Properties — Notes
resources.content:
📖 PERIODIC TABLE & PROPERTIES

Trends across a period (L→R):
• Atomic radius: decreases
• Ionization energy: increases
• Electronegativity: increases
• Electron affinity: generally increases

Trends down a group:
• Atomic radius: increases
• IE: decreases
• EN: decreases
• Metallic character: increases

Exceptions:
• IE: N > O (half-filled stability)
• EA: F < Cl (small size, e⁻ repulsion)
• Noble gases: very high IE, ~0 EA

resources.title: Atomic Structure — Notes
resources.content:
📖 ATOMIC STRUCTURE

Bohr Model: E_n = -13.6Z²/n² eV
Radius: r_n = 0.529n²/Z Å

Quantum Numbers:
• n: principal (1,2,3...)
• l: azimuthal (0 to n-1)
• m_l: magnetic (-l to +l)
• m_s: spin (±½)

Orbitals: s(l=0), p(l=1), d(l=2), f(l=3)
Aufbau: 1s 2s 2p 3s 3p 4s 3d 4p...
Hund Rule: max multiplicity first
Pauli: no two e⁻ same 4 quantum numbers

de Broglie: λ = h/(mv)
Heisenberg: Δx·Δp ≥ h/(4π)

Nodes: radial = n-l-1, angular = l

resources.title: Chemical Thermodynamics — Notes
resources.content:
📖 CHEMICAL THERMODYNAMICS

1st Law: ΔU = q + w
Enthalpy: H = U + PV, ΔH = qp

Hess Law: ΔH is path independent
Bond dissociation: ΔH = Σ(BE reactants) - Σ(BE products)

Entropy: ΔS = q_rev/T
2nd Law: ΔS_universe ≥ 0

Gibbs Energy: ΔG = ΔH - TΔS
• ΔG < 0: spontaneous
• ΔG = 0: equilibrium
• ΔG > 0: non-spontaneous

ΔG° = -RTlnK = -nFE°cell

Cp - Cv = R (ideal gas)
Kirchhoff: ΔH₂ = ΔH₁ + ΔCp(T₂-T₁)

resources.title: Chemical Kinetics — Notes
resources.content:
📖 CHEMICAL KINETICS

Rate = -d[R]/dt = d[P]/dt
Rate law: r = k[A]^m[B]^n
Order = m+n

Zero order: [A] = [A₀] - kt, t½ = [A₀]/(2k)
First order: ln[A] = ln[A₀] - kt, t½ = 0.693/k
Second order: 1/[A] = 1/[A₀] + kt, t½ = 1/(k[A₀])

Arrhenius: k = Ae^(-Ea/RT)
ln(k₂/k₁) = Ea/R(1/T₁ - 1/T₂)

Catalyst lowers Ea
Molecularity vs Order: molecularity is theoretical, order is experimental

resources.title: Electrochemistry — Notes
resources.content:
📖 ELECTROCHEMISTRY

Nernst: E = E° - (RT/nF)lnQ
At 25°C: E = E° - (0.0591/n)logQ

E°cell = E°cathode - E°anode
ΔG° = -nFE°

Faraday Laws:
1st: m = ZIt = MIt/(nF)
2nd: m₁/m₂ = E₁/E₂

F = 96500 C/mol

Kohlrausch: Λ°m = Σλ°(cations) + Σλ°(anions)
Molar conductivity: Λm = κ/c

Conductance = 1/R
Cell constant = l/A

resources.title: Hydrocarbons — Notes
resources.content:
📖 HYDROCARBONS

Alkanes (CₙH₂ₙ₊₂): sp³, σ bonds only
• Reactions: combustion, halogenation (free radical)

Alkenes (CₙH₂ₙ): sp², C=C
• Addition reactions: H₂, HX, H₂O, X₂
• Markovnikov rule: H to more H-bearing C
• Anti-Markovnikov: HBr + peroxide

Alkynes (CₙH₂ₙ₋₂): sp, C≡C
• Acidic H (terminal)
• Addition: 2 equiv of reagent

Arenes: Benzene is aromatic
• EAS: halogenation, nitration, sulfonation, Friedel-Crafts
• Directive effects: ortho-para (activating) vs meta (deactivating)

resources.title: Haloalkanes & Haloarenes — Notes
resources.content:
📖 HALOALKANES & HALOARENES

SN1: 3° > 2° > 1°, carbocation intermediate, racemization
SN2: 1° > 2° > 3°, backside attack, Walden inversion
E1: unimolecular, carbocation, Saytzeff
E2: bimolecular, anti-periplanar, Saytzeff

Reactivity: R-I > R-Br > R-Cl > R-F

Grignard: RMgX + CO₂ → RCOOH
Wurtz: 2RX + 2Na → R-R

Haloarenes: less reactive due to resonance
Sandmeyer reaction for ArX preparation

resources.title: Alcohols, Phenols & Ethers — Notes
resources.content:
📖 ALCOHOLS, PHENOLS & ETHERS

Alcohols (R-OH):
• Acidity: PhOH > H₂O > ROH
• Reactions: dehydration, oxidation, ester formation
• Lucas test: 3° instant, 2° 5min, 1° no rxn

Phenols:
• More acidic than alcohols (resonance stabilization)
• EAS: activated ring, ortho-para directing
• Reimer-Tiemann: salicylaldehyde
• Kolbe: salicylic acid

Ethers (R-O-R):
• Williamson synthesis: RONa + RX → ROR
• Cleavage with HI

resources.title: Aldehydes, Ketones & Acids — Notes
resources.content:
📖 ALDEHYDES, KETONES & CARBOXYLIC ACIDS

Nucleophilic Addition (C=O):
• HCN → cyanohydrin
• NaHSO₃ → bisulphite addition
• Grignard → alcohol

Aldol condensation: base-catalyzed
Cannizzaro: no α-H aldehydes
Tollen test: Ag mirror (aldehyde only)
Fehling test: red ppt (aldehyde only)

Carboxylic Acids:
• Acidity: RCOOH > PhOH > ROH
• Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky: α-halogenation
• Decarboxylation: RCOONa + NaOH → RH
• Ester: RCOOH + ROH → RCOOR (Fischer)

resources.title: Chemical Bonding — Notes
resources.content:
📖 CHEMICAL BONDING

Ionic: transfer of electrons, high MP/BP
Covalent: sharing of electrons

VSEPR Theory:
• AB₂: linear (180°)
• AB₃: trigonal (120°)
• AB₄: tetrahedral (109.5°)
• AB₅: trigonal bipyramidal
• AB₆: octahedral (90°)

Hybridization: sp(180°), sp²(120°), sp³(109.5°)
sp³d(TBP), sp³d²(octahedral)

MO Theory:
Bond order = (bonding - antibonding)/2
O₂ is paramagnetic (BO=2)

H-bonding: F-H...F strongest
Dipole moment: μ = q × d

resources.title: Coordination Compounds — Notes
resources.content:
📖 COORDINATION COMPOUNDS

Terminology:
• Ligand: electron pair donor
• Coordination number: bonds to metal
• Chelate: polydentate ligand ring

Isomerism:
• Geometric (cis-trans)
• Optical (mirror images)
• Ionization, Linkage, Hydrate

CFT (Crystal Field Theory):
• Octahedral: t₂g and eg splitting
• Tetrahedral: smaller splitting
• CFSE determines color, magnetism

Spectrochemical series:
I⁻ < Br⁻ < Cl⁻ < F⁻ < OH⁻ < H₂O < NH₃ < en < CN⁻ < CO

resources.title: p-Block Elements — Notes
resources.content:
📖 p-BLOCK ELEMENTS

Group 13 (B family): B₂H₆ (diborane), BF₃ (Lewis acid)
Group 14 (C family): CO₂, SiO₂, allotropes of carbon
Group 15 (N family): NH₃, HNO₃, N₂O₅
Group 16 (O family): O₃, H₂SO₄, SO₂
Group 17 (Halogens): HF, Cl₂, interhalogen compounds
Group 18 (Noble gases): XeF₂, XeF₄, XeF₆

Key trends:
• Oxidation states: max = group number
• Acidic strength of oxoacids varies
• Anomalous behavior of first member

Important compounds: Borax, Silicones, Phosphine, Ozone

resources.title: Quadratic Equations — Notes
resources.content:
📖 QUADRATIC EQUATIONS

General form: ax² + bx + c = 0

Roots: x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a

Discriminant D = b²-4ac:
• D > 0: Two real distinct roots
• D = 0: Two equal real roots
• D < 0: Two complex roots

Vieta's formulas:
• Sum of roots: α+β = -b/a
• Product of roots: αβ = c/a

Nature of roots:
• D > 0 & D is perfect square → rational roots
• D > 0 & D not perfect square → irrational roots

If α,β are roots: ax²+bx+c = a(x-α)(x-β)

resources.title: Differentiation — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 DIFFERENTIATION

Basic Rules:
• d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹
• d/dx(eˣ) = eˣ
• d/dx(ln x) = 1/x
• d/dx(sin x) = cos x
• d/dx(cos x) = -sin x
• d/dx(tan x) = sec²x

Chain Rule: d/dx[f(g(x))] = f'(g(x))·g'(x)
Product Rule: d/dx(uv) = u'v + uv'
Quotient Rule: d/dx(u/v) = (u'v - uv')/v²

Applications:
• Tangent slope: dy/dx at point
• Maxima/Minima: f'(x) = 0, check f''(x)
• Rate of change
• Increasing: f'(x) > 0
• Decreasing: f'(x) < 0

resources.title: Integration — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 INDEFINITE INTEGRATION

Basic Rules:
• ∫xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) + C
• ∫eˣ dx = eˣ + C
• ∫1/x dx = ln|x| + C
• ∫sin x dx = -cos x + C
• ∫cos x dx = sin x + C

Techniques:
1. Substitution: ∫f(g(x))g'(x)dx = ∫f(u)du
2. By Parts: ∫u dv = uv - ∫v du (LIATE rule)
3. Partial Fractions: for P(x)/Q(x)

LIATE priority:
L - Logarithmic
I - Inverse trig
A - Algebraic
T - Trigonometric
E - Exponential

resources.title: Trigonometry — Key Identities
resources.content:
📋 TRIGONOMETRIC IDENTITIES

Fundamental:
• sin²θ + cos²θ = 1
• 1 + tan²θ = sec²θ
• 1 + cot²θ = cosec²θ

Compound Angles:
• sin(A±B) = sinA·cosB ± cosA·sinB
• cos(A±B) = cosA·cosB ∓ sinA·sinB
• tan(A±B) = (tanA ± tanB)/(1 ∓ tanA·tanB)

Double Angle:
• sin2A = 2sinA·cosA
• cos2A = cos²A - sin²A = 1 - 2sin²A = 2cos²A - 1
• tan2A = 2tanA/(1-tan²A)

Triangle:
• Sine rule: a/sinA = b/sinB = c/sinC = 2R
• Cosine rule: c² = a² + b² - 2ab·cosC
• Area = ½ab·sinC

resources.title: Conic Sections — Key Formulas
resources.content:
📋 CONIC SECTIONS

Parabola: y² = 4ax
• Focus: (a, 0)
• Directrix: x = -a
• Latus rectum: 4a

Ellipse: x²/a² + y²/b² = 1
• e = √(1 - b²/a²) < 1
• Foci: (±ae, 0)
• Latus rectum: 2b²/a

Hyperbola: x²/a² - y²/b² = 1
• e = √(1 + b²/a²) > 1
• Foci: (±ae, 0)
• Asymptotes: y = ±(b/a)x

Circle: x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0
• Centre: (-g, -f)
• Radius: √(g² + f² - c)

resources.title: Probability — Key Concepts
resources.content:
📖 PROBABILITY

Basic: P(A) = n(A)/n(S)

Addition: P(A∪B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A∩B)
For mutually exclusive: P(A∪B) = P(A) + P(B)

Conditional: P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B)

Bayes' Theorem:
P(Aᵢ|B) = P(B|Aᵢ)·P(Aᵢ) / ΣP(B|Aⱼ)·P(Aⱼ)

Binomial Distribution:
P(X=r) = ⁿCᵣ · pʳ · qⁿ⁻ʳ
Mean = np, Variance = npq

Independent events: P(A∩B) = P(A)·P(B)

resources.title: Complex Numbers — Notes
resources.content:
📖 COMPLEX NUMBERS

i = √(-1), i²=-1, i³=-i, i⁴=1
z = a+bi, |z| = √(a²+b²)
Conjugate: z̄ = a-bi

Polar: z = r(cosθ+isinθ) = re^(iθ)
De Moivre: (cosθ+isinθ)ⁿ = cos(nθ)+isin(nθ)
Roots of unity: zₖ = e^(2πik/n)
Triangle inequality: |z₁+z₂| ≤ |z₁|+|z₂|

resources.title: Binomial Theorem — Notes
resources.content:
📖 BINOMIAL THEOREM

(a+b)ⁿ = Σ ⁿCᵣ aⁿ⁻ʳ bʳ
General term: T(r+1) = ⁿCᵣ aⁿ⁻ʳ bʳ
Middle term: T((n+2)/2) if n even

ⁿCᵣ = n!/(r!(n-r)!)
ⁿC₀+ⁿC₁+...+ⁿCₙ = 2ⁿ

For rational index:
(1+x)ⁿ = 1+nx+n(n-1)x²/2!+... for |x|<1

resources.title: Sequences & Series — Notes
resources.content:
📖 SEQUENCES & SERIES

AP: aₙ = a+(n-1)d, Sₙ = n/2[2a+(n-1)d]
GP: aₙ = arⁿ⁻¹, Sₙ = a(rⁿ-1)/(r-1), S∞ = a/(1-r)

AM = (a+b)/2, GM = √(ab), HM = 2ab/(a+b)
AM ≥ GM ≥ HM

Σn = n(n+1)/2
Σn² = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6
Σn³ = [n(n+1)/2]²

resources.title: Matrices & Determinants — Notes
resources.content:
📖 MATRICES & DETERMINANTS

(AB)ᵀ = BᵀAᵀ, (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹
|AB| = |A|·|B|, |kA| = kⁿ|A| (n×n)
A⁻¹ = adj(A)/|A|

Cramer Rule: x = Dₓ/D
Row swap → sign change
Two identical rows → |A| = 0

resources.title: Permutations & Combinations — Notes
resources.content:
📖 PERMUTATIONS & COMBINATIONS

nPr = n!/(n-r)!
nCr = n!/(r!(n-r)!)
Circular permutation: (n-1)!
With repetition: n!/(p!q!r!...)

nCr = nCn-r
nCr+nCr-1 = (n+1)Cr

Derangements: D(n) = n![1-1/1!+1/2!-1/3!+...]
Distribution: n identical into r distinct: (n+r-1)C(r-1)

resources.title: Limits & Continuity — Notes
resources.content:
📖 LIMITS & CONTINUITY

lim sinx/x = 1 as x tends to 0
lim (ex-1)/x = 1 as x tends to 0
lim ln(1+x)/x = 1 as x tends to 0
lim (1+1/x)^x = e as x tends to infinity

L Hopital Rule: if 0/0 or infinity/infinity then differentiate top and bottom

Continuity at x=a: f(a) defined, limit exists, both equal
Sandwich theorem, IVT

resources.title: Differential Equations — Notes
resources.content:
📖 DIFFERENTIAL EQUATIONS

Order = highest derivative, Degree = its power

Types:
1. Variable separable: dy/dx = f(x)g(y)
2. Homogeneous: put y=vx
3. Linear: dy/dx+Py=Q, IF=e^(integral Pdx)
   y*IF = integral(Q*IF dx) + C
4. Exact: Mdx+Ndy=0

resources.title: Straight Lines — Notes
resources.content:
📖 STRAIGHT LINES

y = mx+c, y-y1 = m(x-x1)
x/a + y/b = 1

Angle: tan theta = |m1-m2|/(1+m1*m2)
Parallel: m1=m2, Perpendicular: m1*m2=-1

Distance from point to line: |ax1+by1+c|/sqrt(a2+b2)
Family: L1+lambda*L2 = 0

resources.title: Circles — Notes
resources.content:
📖 CIRCLES

(x-h)2+(y-k)2=r2
General: x2+y2+2gx+2fy+c=0
Centre(-g,-f), radius sqrt(g2+f2-c)

Tangent length = sqrt(S1)
Radical axis: S1-S2=0
Orthogonal: 2g1*g2+2f1*f2=c1+c2

resources.title: Vectors — Notes
resources.content:
📖 VECTORS

Dot product: a.b = |a||b|cos theta
Cross product: axb = |a||b|sin theta * n_hat
|axb| = area of parallelogram

Projection of a on b: (a.b)/|b|
Scalar triple product: [a b c] = volume
Coplanar if [a b c] = 0

resources.title: 3D Geometry — Notes
resources.content:
📖 3D GEOMETRY

Line: (x-x1)/a = (y-y1)/b = (z-z1)/c
Plane: ax+by+cz=d
Distance from point: |ax1+by1+cz1-d|/sqrt(a2+b2+c2)

Angle between planes: cos theta = |n1.n2|/(|n1||n2|)
Skew line dist: |[b1xb2].(a2-a1)|/|b1xb2|

resources.title: Statistics — Notes
resources.content: 📖 STATISTICS\\n\\nMean = Sum xi / n\\nVariance = Sum(xi-xbar)2 / n\\nSD = sqrt(variance)\\n\\nCV = (sigma/xbar)*100\\nRange = max-min\\nQuartiles: Q1, Q2(median), Q3\\nIQR = Q3-Q1

Explain kinematics in Telugu

📖 Kinematics — Study of Motion

🔑 Key Equations:
• v = u + at
• s = ut + ½at²
• v² = u² + 2as
• s = ½(u+v)t

Where: u = initial velocity, v = final velocity, a = acceleration, t = time, s = displacement

⚡ JEE Focus:
• Relative motion problems
• Projectile motion (split into x & y components)
• Graphs: slope of x-t = velocity, slope of v-t = acceleration

📌 To practice: Go to Subjects → Physics → Kinematics chapter for resources & PYQs!

🎯 Most asked: Projectile at angle θ → time of flight = 2u·sinθ/g, Range = u²·sin2θ/g

Explain kinematics in Telugu

📖 కైనమాటిక్స్ — చలనం యొక్క అధ్యయనం

🔑 ముఖ్యమైన సూత్రాలు:
• v = u + at
• s = ut + ½at²
• v² = u² + 2as

⚡ JEE ఫోకస్: సాపేక్ష చలనం (Relative motion), ప్రక్షేపకం (Projectile).
Explain calculus in Telugu

📖 కాలిక్యులస్ 

🔑 భేద నియమాలు: 
• పవర్: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹ 
• చైన్: d/dx[f(g(x))] = f'(g(x))·g'(x) 
• ఉత్పత్తి: d/dx(uv) = u'v + uv' 
• గుణకం: d/dx(u/v) = (u'v - uv')/v² 

🔑 ఇంటిగ్రేషన్ టెక్నిక్స్: 
1. ప్రత్యామ్నాయం: ∫f(g(x))g'(x)dx 
2. భాగాల ద్వారా: ∫udv = uv - ∫vdu (LIATE నియమం) 
3. పాక్షిక భిన్నాలు: హేతుబద్ధమైన విధుల కోసం 

📐 ముఖ్య ఫలితాలు: 
• ∫sinx dx = -cosx + C 
• ∫eˣ dx = eˣ + C 
• ∫1/x dx = ln|x| + సి 

📌 సబ్జెక్ట్‌లు → గణితం → వనరులు & PYQల కోసం కాలిక్యులస్‌కి వెళ్లండి! 

🎯 కాలిక్యులస్ = JEE గణితంలో ~20%. చాలా ముఖ్యమైనది!
Explain calculus in Malyalam

📖 Calculus

🔑 Differentiation Rules:
• Power: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹
• Chain: d/dx[f(g(x))] = f'(g(x))·g'(x)
• Product: d/dx(uv) = u'v + uv'
• Quotient: d/dx(u/v) = (u'v - uv')/v²

🔑 Integration Techniques:
1. Substitution: ∫f(g(x))g'(x)dx
2. By Parts: ∫udv = uv - ∫vdu (LIATE rule)
3. Partial Fractions: for rational functions

📐 Key Results:
• ∫sinx dx = -cosx + C
• ∫eˣ dx = eˣ + C
• ∫1/x dx = ln|x| + C

📌 Go to Subjects → Mathematics → Calculus for resources & PYQs!

🎯 Calculus = ~20% of JEE Math. Very important!

🃏 Quiz me

🃏 Flash Quiz!

📝 A particle moves along a straight line such that its displacement at any time t is given by s = t³ - 6t² + 3t + 4. The velocity when the acceleration is zero is:

A) -9 m/s
B) -12 m/s
C) 3 m/s
D) 42 m/s

⏰ Think about it, then scroll down for the answer...







✅ Answer: A
📖 a = 6t - 12 = 0 → t = 2. v = 3t² - 12t + 3 = 12 - 24 + 3 = -9 m/s

🃏 Flash Quiz (5 Questions)!

📝 Q1: A particle moves along a straight line such that its displacement at any time t is given by s = t³ - 6t² + 3t + 4. The velocity when the acceleration is zero is:

A) -9 m/s
B) -12 m/s
C) 3 m/s
D) 42 m/s

---

📝 Q2: A ball is thrown vertically upward with velocity 20 m/s from the top of a building 40 m high. The time after which the ball hits the ground is (g = 10 m/s²):

A) 2 s
B) 4 s
C) 2(1+√2) s
D) 2+2√2 s

---

📝 Q3: Two blocks of masses 5 kg and 10 kg are connected by a string passing over a frictionless pulley. The acceleration of the system is (g = 10 m/s²):

A) 10/3 m/s²
B) 5/3 m/s²
C) g/3 m/s²
D) g/6 m/s²

---

📝 Q4: A body of mass 2 kg has kinetic energy 8 J. A constant force of 4 N is applied on it in the direction of motion. The kinetic energy after 3 m displacement is:

A) 20 J
B) 12 J
C) 16 J
D) 24 J

---

📝 Q5: Two point charges +3μC and -3μC are placed 20 cm apart. The electric potential at the midpoint of the line joining them is:

A) 0 V
B) 2.7 × 10⁵ V
C) -2.7 × 10⁵ V
D) 5.4 × 10⁵ V

---

⏰ Think about it, then scroll down for the answers...







✅ Answers:

Q1: A
📖 a = 6t - 12 = 0 → t = 2. v = 3t² - 12t + 3 = 12 - 24 + 3 = -9 m/s

Q2: B
📖 -40 = 20t - 5t². 5t² - 20t - 40 = 0. t² - 4t - 8 = 0. t = (4+√48)/2 = 4s (taking positive root)

Q3: C
📖 a = (m₂-m₁)g/(m₁+m₂) = (10-5)×10/(10+5) = 50/15 = 10/3 = g/3 m/s²

Q4: A
📖 Work = Fd = 4×3 = 12J. Final KE = initial KE + Work = 8 + 12 = 20 J

Q5: A
📖 At midpoint, distance from each charge = 10 cm. V = kq₁/r + kq₂/r = k(3-3)/0.1 = 0

Feeling stressed

It sounds like things are feeling really heavy right now. I hear you. 🫂

When the pressure of preparation mounts up, it's easy to feel lost. Let's take a step back and reset your mind.

Here is a quick circuit breaker for your stress:

1. Look around you and name 3 things you can see.
2. Unclench your jaw and drop your shoulders. 🧘
3. Splash some cold water on your face.
4. Step outside or look out a window for 5 minutes.

Right now, your mind is racing. Let's slow it down:

• You don't need to finish the whole syllabus today.
• Taking a break is not a waste of time; it's a necessary recharge.
• Your mental health is far more important than any test score.

When you're ready, try writing down exactly what's bothering you. Is it a specific subject? A pending deadline? Fear of the outcome?
Let me know if you want to talk about it, or if you'd rather we do a fun Quiz or Pomodoro session to distract you!
Feeling stressed

Take a deep breath. 🌬️ I'm here for you.

It is completely normal to feel burnt out and stressed when you're working so hard for a massive goal.

Let's do a quick 'Grounding Exercise' to get you out of the stress loop:

1. 🦶 Plant your feet firmly on the floor.
2. 🌬️ Breathe in deeply for 4 seconds, hold for 4, exhale for 6. (Repeat this 3 times).
3. 💧 Go drink a full glass of water.
4. 🛌 Rest your eyes by looking at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Remember this:

• The effort you are putting in right now is already making you stronger.
• You don't have to be perfect; you just have to be consistent.
• Give yourself permission to have a bad day. It's okay.

What do you need right now?

• We can map out a study plan for just the next 1 hour.
• We can review a simple topic to build your confidence.
• Or we can just talk about what's making you anxious. I'm all ears!
